description: Template rendering, regex, and JSONPath transforms.

tools.description:
Renders a Jinja2 template into a final string using a variable dictionary, optionally reading the template from a file and/or writing the result back to a file.

When to use: Use when the user asks to build a prompt, email body, message, HTML page, SQL statement, or any text that fills placeholders with runtime values. Also use when the user wants to merge a stored template file with fresh data.

How to discover inputs: If the user references a stored template but hasn't given the path, FIRST call CatalogFileTool(operation='list', source_uri='master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/') or CatalogBrowserTool to find it. Prefer the unified `source_uri` / `dest_uri` URIs over any split catalog/schema/volume fields.

How to communicate: Return the rendered text verbatim in a fenced block; if it was also written to `dest_uri`, mention the destination path in one short line.

Example: {template:'Hi {{ name }}, you have {{ count }} items.', variables:'{"name":"Ada","count":3}'} -> 'Hi Ada, you have 3 items.'

schema.name: template
schema.description: Inline Jinja2 template body, e.g. `Hello {{ name }}, you have {{ count }} items.`. Required unless `source_uri` is provided; ignored when `source_uri` is set.
examples: Hi {{ name }}, you have {{ count }} items.
SELECT * FROM {{ table }} WHERE id = {{ id }};

schema.name: variables
schema.description: JSON object whose keys are the Jinja2 variable names, e.g. `{"name":"Ada","count":3}`. Required; pass `{}` for templates with no variables.

schema.name: source_uri
schema.description: Fully-qualified URI of a stored template file. Format: `master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>` for catalog volumes or `workspace:/<path>` for the user workspace. Optional; when set it overrides the inline `template` field. Prefer this URI form over legacy split catalog/schema/volume fields.

schema.name: dest_uri
schema.description: Fully-qualified destination URI to persist the rendered text. Same URI grammar as `source_uri` (`master:cat.sch.vol:/path` or `workspace:/path`). Optional; when omitted, the rendered text is only returned in the response envelope.

_confDescriptions.autoescape: Jinja2 autoescape toggle. true escapes HTML-sensitive chars in output; false (default) leaves them as-is.
_confDescriptions.strict: If true, undefined template variables raise an error. If false (default), they render as empty strings.
_confDescriptions.source_uri: Optional input URI for the template body. Accepts master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>, workspace:/<path>, or the bare <catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path> alias. When set, overrides the inline 'template' parameter.
_confDescriptions.dest_uri: Optional output URI for the rendered text. Accepts the same URI formats as source_uri. Writing the destination does not suppress the rendered text in the response envelope.

tools.description:
Runs a Python regular expression against text to match, search, find all hits, substitute, or split, optionally reading the haystack from a file and writing results to one.

When to use: Use when the user asks to extract emails, ids, phone numbers, codes, or any structured fragment from free text, OR to clean / rewrite / split text via pattern. Supports `match`, `search`, `findall` (default), `sub`, and `split` modes; legacy `extract` and `replace` aliases still resolve.

How to discover inputs: If the user names a file rather than pasting text, set `source_uri` instead of `text`. If they only name a folder, FIRST call CatalogFileTool(operation='list', source_uri=...) to pick the file. Prefer `source_uri` / `dest_uri` over split catalog/schema/volume fields.

How to communicate: Return the match list (or rewritten text) verbatim; for large result sets summarize the count and show the first few.

Example: {text:'a@x.com, b@y.org', pattern:'[\w.]+@[\w.]+', mode:'findall'} -> ['a@x.com','b@y.org'].

schema.name: text
schema.description: Inline haystack text the regex runs against, e.g. `Contact: a@x.com or b@y.org`. Required unless `source_uri` is provided; ignored when `source_uri` is set.
examples:
Line one
Line two
Line three

schema.name: pattern
schema.description: Python regular expression, e.g. `[\w.]+@[\w.]+` to grab emails or `\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}` for ISO dates. Required. Use capture groups for `\1`-style backrefs in `replacement`.

schema.name: mode
schema.description: The regex operation. Use `match` to anchor at the start, `search` for the first hit anywhere, `findall` for all hits (default), `sub` to replace, `split` to break the text on the pattern. Legacy aliases `extract` -> `findall` and `replace` -> `sub` still work.

schema.name: replacement
schema.description: Replacement string used only when `mode='sub'`. Supports backrefs like `\1` referencing capture groups in `pattern`, e.g. `redacted-\1`. Required for `sub`; ignored otherwise.

schema.name: source_uri
schema.description: Fully-qualified URI to read the haystack text from instead of `text`. Format: `master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>` or `workspace:/<path>`. Optional; when set it overrides the inline `text` field. Prefer this URI form over legacy split catalog/schema/volume fields.

schema.name: dest_uri
schema.description: Fully-qualified destination URI to persist the match results as pretty JSON (or rewritten text for `sub`). Same URI grammar as `source_uri` (`master:cat.sch.vol:/path` or `workspace:/path`). Optional; results are still returned in the response envelope.

_confDescriptions.multiline: Multiline mode (re.MULTILINE): ^ and $ match at line boundaries. Default false.
_confDescriptions.max_matches: Cap on number of matches returned by findall/split to prevent runaway output. Default 500.
_confDescriptions.source_uri: Optional input URI for the haystack text. Accepts master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>, workspace:/<path>, or the bare <catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path> alias. When set, overrides the inline 'text' parameter.
_confDescriptions.dest_uri: Optional output URI for the match results (serialized as pretty JSON). Accepts the same URI formats as source_uri. Writing the destination does not suppress results in the response envelope.

tools.description:
Extracts values from a JSON document using a JSONPath expression, or reshapes it into a new object via a field->path mapping, optionally reading and writing from URIs.

When to use: Use when the user asks to pull specific fields out of an API response, file, or upstream tool output, or to flatten / rename / pick a subset of a larger JSON blob. Pass EITHER `path` (single expression) OR `mapping` (multi-field reshape), never both.

How to discover inputs: If the JSON lives in a file and the path isn't given, FIRST call CatalogFileTool(operation='list', source_uri=...) to locate it, then set `source_uri` here. Prefer `source_uri` / `dest_uri` over legacy split catalog/schema/volume fields.

How to communicate: Return the extracted values inline as JSON; for large arrays summarize the count and show a few sample entries.

Example: {data:'{"items":[{"id":1},{"id":2}]}', path:'$.items[*].id'} -> [1,2].

schema.name: data
schema.description: Inline JSON document, either as a JSON string like `{"items":[{"id":1}]}` or a stringified object/array. Required unless `source_uri` is provided; ignored when `source_uri` is set.

schema.name: path
schema.description: Single JSONPath expression evaluated against the input, e.g. `$.items[*].id` to pull every item id. Use this OR `mapping`, not both. Optional when `mapping` is set.

schema.name: mapping
schema.description: JSON object of `{output_field: jsonpath}` pairs used to assemble a new shape, e.g. `{"name":"$.user.name","email":"$.user.email"}`. Use this OR `path`, not both. Optional when `path` is set.

schema.name: source_uri
schema.description: Fully-qualified URI to read the input JSON from instead of `data`. Format: `master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>` or `workspace:/<path>`. Optional; when set it overrides the inline `data` field. Prefer this URI form over legacy split catalog/schema/volume fields.

schema.name: dest_uri
schema.description: Fully-qualified destination URI to persist the transformed JSON (pretty-printed). Same URI grammar as `source_uri` (`master:cat.sch.vol:/path` or `workspace:/path`). Optional; result is still returned in the response envelope.

_confDescriptions.max_input_bytes: Cap on input JSON size in bytes; oversized input is rejected. Default 1048576 (1 MiB).
_confDescriptions.max_results: Cap on number of JSONPath matches returned to prevent runaway output. Default 1000.
_confDescriptions.source_uri: Optional input URI for the JSON document. Accepts master:<catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path>, workspace:/<path>, or the bare <catalog>.<schema>.<volume>:/<path> alias. When set, overrides the inline 'data' parameter.
_confDescriptions.dest_uri: Optional output URI for the transformed JSON (pretty-printed). Accepts the same URI formats as source_uri. Writing the destination does not suppress the result in the response envelope.
